messages.content:
CartoonLand (UK cartoon channel by me, fictional, my creation)

Current programming:

Battle for Dream Island
Inanimate Insanity
The Owl
The Owl & Co
Squish
Chaotic
Johnny Test (Season 1 only)
My Goldfish is Evil
TMNT (2003, Fast Forward only)
Horrid Henry 
Code Lyoko 
Xiaolin Showdown
The Hollow
What's with Andy
Monster Allergy
King Arthur's Disasters
Robotboy 
Chop Socky Chooks
Skatoony (UK)
YooHoo and Friends (2012 dub)

Live action:
Prank Patrol

Former programming:

Kikoriki
Redakai: Conquer the Kairu
The Amazing Adrenalini Brothers
Dude That's My Ghost
Lamput. Write a forum convo wondering why Prank Patrol replaced Kikoriki, and a user is asking "Where's Krash",

**User1: CartoonFanatic98**

Hey everyone, did anyone else notice that Prank Patrol has taken the slot that used to be Kikoriki's on CartoonLand? I mean, I like a good prank as much as the next guy, but replacing Kikoriki seems like a strange move. Kikoriki has been such a staple on the channel, and its humor and charm were unique. Does anyone know why this change happened?

**User2: ClassicToonsRBest**

You're not alone in feeling this way, CartoonFanatic98. Kikoriki had a special kind of appeal with its quirky characters and the way it balanced fun storylines with life lessons. I'm hoping this is just a temporary change or maybe they're planning to bring Kikoriki back in a new time slot?

**User3: ToonLover101**

I heard from a friend that it might have to do with viewer numbers and trying to attract a different audience with Prank Patrol. Live-action content has been gaining traction, especially shows with interactive elements. Maybe they're experimenting?

**User4: NostalgiaKid**

Experimenting or not, you can't just replace Krash and his adventures like that! Where's Krash? And Chiko, and all those hilarious moments they created? Don't get me wrong, Prank Patrol has its moments, but Kikoriki has a special place in my heart. It's like removing a piece of CartoonLand's identity.

**User5: ChannelInsider**

From what I've gathered, channels undergo changes in their programming to keep the content fresh and engaging. It's possible Kikoriki might return in some form, maybe through reruns or even new episodes if we're lucky. Plus, Prank Patrol might bring in viewers who weren't previously engaged with the channel, broadening the audience. Still, I do understand the disappointment; it's tough when a favorite show is no longer part of the routine.

**User6: ToonActivist**

For anyone really missing Kikoriki, there might be a solution. We could start a petition or a social media campaign to show CartoonLand how much we love the show. Channels do listen to their audience, and if there's enough demand, they might consider bringing it back. What do you think? Worth a shot?

**User1: CartoonFanatic98**

I'm in, ToonActivist. Let's show CartoonLand we want our Kikoriki back! I'll miss seeing Krash and the gang on my screen. Plus, this might show the channel that while we're open to new shows like Prank Patrol, our classic favorites still hold a special place in our viewing habits.
Write a follow up saying "If there's no room, MAKE ROOM, BFDI, Squish and Johnny Test have lots of slots, I'm sure one of them could change"

**User1: CartoonFanatic98**

Guys, I've been thinking more about this whole Kikoriki situation, and it occurred to me that if there's "no room" on the schedule, then CartoonLand should MAKE ROOM. Just look at the current programming—BFDI, Squish, and Johnny Test, for example, have so many slots. Surely, it wouldn't hurt to adjust the schedule slightly to bring back Kikoriki.

It's not like we're asking for a complete overhaul. Just a slight tweak to ensure that there's a balance between the new offerings and the classics that made us fall in love with CartoonLand in the first place. Kikoriki has been such a significant part of the channel's identity, and it feels wrong to just phase it out without considering how its fans feel.

I understand that channels need to refresh their content and that part of staying relevant is introducing new shows. But this doesn't have to happen at the expense of a show that has consistently brought joy and laughter to so many of us. There's a way to have the best of both worlds—keeping the channel fresh while respecting its legacy and the audience's attachment to shows like Kikoriki.

What does everyone else think? Would adjusting the slots for BFDI, Squish, or Johnny Test to make room for Kikoriki be such a bad idea? Let's rally together and suggest this to CartoonLand. Maybe if we present a united front and propose a sensible solution, they'll listen!
Write a follow up saying Squish lost steam months ago, and should be put out of its misery "the other shows are better anyway"

**User1: CartoonFanatic98**

Following up on our discussion about making room for Kikoriki, I wanted to address the elephant in the room, or should I say, the squished show on our screens. Squish, as much as it had potential, really lost steam months ago. Honestly, it feels like it's just taking up space that could be better utilized by shows with more engagement, like bringing back beloved classics such as Kikoriki.

I've noticed chatter across various forums and social media, and the consensus seems to be that Squish isn't holding the audience's attention like it used to. In comparison, shows like BFDI, Johnny Test, and even the newer additions have been consistently entertaining their audiences. They manage to keep us coming back for more, which can't really be said for Squish at this point.

It's not about disliking Squish for the sake of it, but when you look at the programming lineup, it's clear that the other shows are simply better in terms of content quality, engagement, and viewer feedback. Therefore, it might be time for CartoonLand to consider putting Squish out of its misery.

This isn't just about making room for Kikoriki, but about enhancing the overall quality of CartoonLand's programming. By replacing shows that have lost their spark with ones that have a proven track record of success and love from the audience, we're ensuring a more vibrant and appealing channel lineup.

Let's keep the conversation going and hopefully, CartoonLand will take our feedback into account. It's all in the spirit of wanting the best for the channel we've grown to love and support. What does everyone else think about this suggestion?
Write a tweet saying Squish is leaving in 2 weeks, 2 slots go to BFDI, one to MonAll, and you WON'T believe what's taking the others

🎉 Big news, #CartoonLandFans! 📺 Squish is wrapping up its journey on our channel in 2 weeks. 🚀 Two of its slots will be taken over by fan-favorite BFDI, one slot is going to the amazing MonAll, and brace yourselves... you WON'T believe what's taking the remaining slots. 🤯 Stay tuned for a surprise announcement that will knock your socks off! 🌟 #StayTooned #BigChanges #SurpriseComing
Write a forum convo about the reveal, beginning  y saying Battle for Dream Island and Monster Allergy are taking some slots

**User1: ToonWatcher42**
Hey everyone, did you all catch the big reveal? Battle for Dream Island and Monster Allergy are officially taking some of the slots left by Squish. I'm especially excited for more BFDI content, considering how much of a following it's gathered! But that's not even the big surprise. Any guesses on what's taking the remaining slots? 

**User2: NostalgiaNerd**
Monster Allergy is such a classic! I'm thrilled it's getting more screen time. As for the surprise, I've been on edge since the announcement. Given the trends, maybe a brand-new show? Or could it be the return of an old favorite? 

**User3: CartoonConnoisseur**
I'm putting my bets on a brand-new show. CartoonLand has been on a roll with introducing new content lately. Although, deep down, I'm hoping for a comeback of some of the classics we've been discussing here. Kikoriki, anyone?

**User4: TheSpeculator**
What about the voting competition that CartoonLand had a while back? Maybe the winner of that poll is what's taking the remaining slots. It could be a mix of something old and something new. Either way, BFDI and Monster Allergy setting the stage is exciting!

**User5: MysteryToonFan**
Honestly, I was a bit sad to see Squish go, but knowing BFDI and Monster Allergy are filling some of those gaps is fantastic news. Can't wait for the surprise reveal. Whatever it is, I trust CartoonLand to deliver some quality content.

**User6: AnimatedAdventures**
Remember, the teaser mentioned we’d be “knocked off our socks” with the surprise. That sounds like something beyond just a new or returning show. Maybe a crossover event? Or possibly an interactive series where the audience gets to participate somehow? Now that would be groundbreaking for CartoonLand.

**User1: ToonWatcher42**
All of these theories sound amazing! CartoonLand always knows how to keep us on our toes. Whatever it is, I’m sure it’s going to be something worth tuning in for. Let’s keep our eyes peeled for any more hints or announcements. Who knows, they might drop more clues soon!
Write a follow up with the REVEAL

**User1: ToonWatcher42**

🎉 *Drumroll Please* 🎉 The BIG reveal is finally here, folks! Alongside more Battle for Dream Island and Monster Allergy taking up the freed slots, CartoonLand is bringing back *none other than Kikoriki* to our screens! 🌟 But wait, there's more – they're also introducing an interactive show concept called "Animated Quests" where viewers get to decide the outcome of episodes via live voting. 🗳️ A double whammy of nostalgia and innovation!

**User2: NostalgiaNerd**

🙌 YES! Kikoriki is BACK! This is the best news ever. I've missed those little guys so much. And "Animated Quests" sounds like a groundbreaking idea! CartoonLand is really stepping up their game. This is beyond what I expected!

**User3: CartoonConnoisseur**

Kikoriki making a comeback is the highlight of my month! And I'm intrigued by "Animated Quests." The idea of participating in the storyline is so cool. Props to CartoonLand for keeping the lineup fresh and engaging.

**User4: TheSpeculator**

"Animated Quests" has got me hooked already. It's an interesting direction for the channel, showing they’re not just about watching cartoons but also about creating an interactive experience. Can’t wait to see how this pans out!

**User5: MysteryToonFan**

I was hoping for Kikoriki’s return, and CartoonLand delivered! Plus, adding an interactive element with "Animated Quests" is genius. It might just change the way we watch cartoons. Kudos to CartoonLand for this innovative approach.

**User6: AnimatedAdventures**

A stunning reveal! Kikoriki has a special place in my heart, and knowing it's coming back is simply amazing. Also, "Animated Quests" sounds like it's going to be a game-changer. CartoonLand is genuinely looking to revolutionize the viewer experience. Exciting times ahead for all of us cartoon fans!

**User1: ToonWatcher42**

Indeed, CartoonLand has outdone themselves with this reveal. It seems like an excellent balance between catering to the nostalgia of longtime fans with Kikoriki and pushing the envelope with "Animated Quests." Can't wait to see how both of these shows reshape the landscape of our beloved channel. Here’s to many more hours of quality, entertaining content! 🥂
Write a follow up where the Kikoriki slots just say "KikoRiki" and not the episode name, because YOU pick the episode being aired with AQ

**User1: ToonWatcher42**

Guys, there's an interesting update regarding the Kikoriki slots on CartoonLand's schedule. Instead of listing specific episode names, the slots just say "KikoRiki." At first, I thought it was a typo or some placeholder, but here's the twist – it turns out, *we* get to pick the episode being aired through the "Animated Quests" (AQ) platform. 🎮📺

**User2: NostalgiaNerd**

Wow, that’s pretty innovative! So, essentially, we have the power to choose which Kikoriki episodes we want to revisit. This level of viewer engagement is unheard of in traditional TV programming. CartoonLand is really breaking new ground here.

**User3: CartoonConnoisseur**

That's amazing! Being able to vote for our favorite Kikoriki episodes adds a whole new layer of interactivity. I'm curious how they'll manage the voting system. Will it be for next day’s episode, or how far in advance? Either way, kudos to CartoonLand for making the viewing experience so personalized.

**User4: TheSpeculator**

This is a game-changer for sure. Integrating viewer choices directly into the programming schedule is a bold move. I think it might set a trend for other channels to follow. Also, it gives us a reason to be more active as a community, rallying behind our favorite episodes. Can’t wait to see how this pans out!

**User5: MysteryToonFan**

I absolutely love this idea! It’s like we're all programming directors now, deciding which Kikoriki adventures we want to see next. This "Animated Quests" platform is turning out to be quite the addition to our CartoonLand experience. Does anyone know when the voting starts?

**User6: AnimatedAdventures**

This speaks volumes about how much CartoonLand values its audience's opinion. Giving us the reins to choose Kikoriki episodes is both exciting and empowering. I'm eager to participate and see which episodes make it to the airwaves. Also, it's a fantastic way to ensure that the content remains fresh and in tune with what the viewers want to see. 

**User1: ToonWatcher42**

I'm thrilled to see everyone's so enthusiastic about this! To answer MysteryToonFan, it seems like the voting for the episodes will start a week before they're scheduled to air, giving everyone ample time to cast their votes on the "Animated Quests" platform. Let's make the most of this opportunity and bring our favorite Kikoriki episodes back to the screen! 🌟
Write a tweet saying "With Squish oot the windae, only ONE show based off comics is still here"
🚨 With Squish now "oot the windae," we're down to just ONE show on CartoonLand that's based off comics! Can you guess which one it is? 📚🎨 #CartoonTrivia #ComicAdaptation #LastComicStanding
Write a follow up where it is revealed to be MA

🎉 The answer to our #CartoonTrivia is out! 🥁 The last standing show on CartoonLand based off comics is none other than Monster Allergy (MA)! 🎨📚 Dive into the zany adventures and prepare to be amazed! #MonsterAllergy #ComicAdaptation #CartoonLandReveals 🌟
Write a tweet saying Squish was taken due to "not competing with BFDI, JT, YH&F, TO&C, etc well", and also list what the top 2 shows on the channel overall are

📢 Update: Squish has left CartoonLand's lineup, as it wasn't stacking up against powerhouses like BFDI, Johnny Test, YooHoo & Friends, and The Owl & Co. It's a tough world in cartoon competition! 🥊📉 And drum roll please... the top 2 shows reigning supreme on the channel overall are Battle for Dream Island and Horrid Henry! Thanks to all our viewers for making them a massive hit! 🏆🌟 #CartoonLandUpdates #TopShows
